workflowDefinitions.name: FireAndForgetTaskWorkflow
workflowDefinitions.description: Lightweight fire-and-forget task delegation. Delegates a single task to the target agent — no result delivery, no notification. Designed for Temporal-scheduled tasks and one-off delegations where the result is visible in the dashboard but doesn't need to be reported back. Prefer TaskDelegationWorkflow when you need guaranteed result delivery to the CTO agent.

steps.name: delegate

workflowDefinitions.name: TaskDelegationWorkflow
workflowDefinitions.description: General-purpose ad-hoc task delegation to any agent. Delegates via DelegateToAgentActivity, waits for response, delivers result to the CTO agent.

steps.name: delegate
notify-result

workflowDefinitions.name: MemoryStoreRequestWorkflow
workflowDefinitions.description: Memory store request — the CTO agent reviews, searches for duplicates, stores/updates/revises/rejects. Back-and-forth loop with requesting agent. No signal gates or StoreMemory activity — the CTO uses memory MCP tools directly.

steps.name: cto_review

REVISE.name: author_revision
REVISE.instruction:
Your memory store request needs revision before it can be persisted.

Your original proposal:
- Title: {{input.Title}}
- Content: {{input.Content}}

Reviewer feedback:
{{vars.review_response}}

Please revise your proposal based on this feedback. If the reviewer suggested updating an existing memory instead, provide the updated content that should be appended. Respond with your revised proposal — include both the title and content.

When incorporating prior consensus or CEO feedback:
- Treat the comment as authoritative. Do not re-argue the design.
- Restate the concern in your own words in a comment on the issue/PR, so reviewers can verify you understood.
- Make the change the comment asks for. If you believe it is wrong, respond via changes_requested on the next round rather than ignoring it.

workflowDefinitions.name: UweDesignToPrWorkflow
workflowDefinitions.description: Thin orchestrator: UweDesignWorkflow child (consensus review + CEO design-approval gate) → UwePrImplementationWorkflow abandoned child. Replaces compiled DesignToPrWorkflow. Input: Description (required), ExistingIssueNumber (optional), Repo (required), DesignAgent (required), ImplementAgent (required), ConsensusAgents (required), MaxConsensusRounds (default 3).

steps.name: notify_complete
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: UweDesignToPrWorkflow completed for issue #{{vars.issue_number}} in {{input.Repo}}. Design approved by consensus and CEO. UwePrImplementationWorkflow launched (workflow ID: {{vars.impl_wf_id}}) — monitor progress in the Temporal dashboard. You will receive a merge-approval signal when the PR is ready for your review.

workflowDefinitions.name: UweDesignWorkflow
workflowDefinitions.description: Creates or refines a well-specified GitHub issue with multi-agent consensus review, then gates on CEO design-approval. Consensus loop (max 3 rounds) — if exhausted or needs_human_review, escalates to CEO gate instead of cancelling. CEO wait loop (hourly reminders, no auto-cancel). Returns {IssueNumber: N} on approval. Replaces compiled DesignWorkflow.

steps.name: create_or_refine
steps.instruction:
Your task is to create or refine a GitHub issue in repo {{input.Repo}}.

Determine which action to take by checking in priority order:
1. Prior issue number from previous iteration: {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)' | default: 'none'}} — if this is a number, REFINE that issue.
2. ExistingIssueNumber from input: {{input.ExistingIssueNumber | default: 'none'}} — if this is a number, REFINE that issue.
3. If neither is set — CREATE a new well-specified GitHub issue for: {{input.Description}}.

For CREATE: use `gh issue create --repo {{input.Repo}}`.
For REFINE: use `gh issue edit <N> --repo {{input.Repo}}`.

Incorporate this prior consensus feedback (if any):
{{vars.consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning | default: 'none'}}

Incorporate this prior CEO feedback (if any):
{{vars.approval_signal.Comment | default: 'none'}}

CEO consensus direction (if any):
{{vars.ceo_consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning | default: 'none'}}

At the end of your response, output exactly: ISSUE_NUMBER: <number>

When incorporating prior consensus or CEO feedback:
- Treat the comment as authoritative. Do not re-argue the design.
- Restate the concern in your own words in a comment on the issue/PR, so reviewers can verify you understood.
- Make the change the comment asks for. If you believe it is wrong, respond via changes_requested on the next round rather than ignoring it.

Every spec MUST include:
- A 'Files to Touch' section enumerating each file/path the implementer will modify.
- An 'Acceptance Criteria' section with testable assertions (not vague goals).
- A 'Constraints / MUST NOT' section listing at least 3 domain-specific dangers the implementation must avoid. Not generic boilerplate — concrete, workflow-specific MUST-NOTs.
- Every external dependency (DB, file, HTTP call, process) must have an explicit failure path stated.

args.Subject: Issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} design spec in {{input.Repo}}
args.ReviewPrompt:
Review the design spec for GitHub issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in repo {{input.Repo}}. Fetch it with: gh issue view {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} --repo {{input.Repo}}

Evaluate whether the spec is complete and unambiguous enough to implement without guessing. Check: are all edge cases covered? Are the code changes described precisely enough for an implementer to follow without design decisions of their own?

Review discipline:
- Fetch the current spec/PR fresh — do NOT summarize prior rounds.
- Verify each prior finding against the current text before reporting.
- Give structured findings with severity (blocker, high, medium, low).
- End your response with exactly one verdict line: VERDICT: approved | changes_requested | needs_human_review.

steps.name: notify_needs_human_consensus
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: DesignWorkflow for issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in {{input.Repo}} — consensus review flagged needs_human_review. Manual intervention required. Reasoning: {{vars.consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning}}

steps.name: emit_needs_human_error
steps.instruction: Output exactly: ERROR:needs_human_review

steps.name: notify_consensus_exhausted
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: DesignWorkflow for issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in {{input.Repo}} exhausted all consensus rounds without approval. Manual intervention required.

steps.name: emit_exhausted_error
steps.instruction: Output exactly: ERROR:consensus_exhausted

notifyStep.instruction:
Post to Telegram group: Design spec for issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} ({{input.Repo}}) approved by consensus and is awaiting your decision.

Context for your decision:
- Issue: #{{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in {{input.Repo}} — run `gh issue view {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} --repo {{input.Repo}}` to review the full spec
- Consensus: reviewers approved the spec as complete and implementable
- approved → implementation starts immediately; changes_requested → spec returns to the design agent for another revision round; rejected → workflow cancelled

Send design-approval signal to workflow `{{config.WorkflowId | default: 'this workflow'}}`:
  {"Decision":"approved"} — approve the design spec
  {"Decision":"changes_requested","Comment":"your feedback"} — request refinement
  {"Decision":"rejected","Comment":"reason"} — cancel

steps.name: notify_ceo_approved
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: CEO approved the design spec for issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in {{input.Repo}}. Setting workflow result and completing.

steps.name: emit_approved_result
steps.instruction: Output exactly this JSON and nothing else: {"IssueNumber": {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}}}

steps.name: notify_rejected
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: DesignWorkflow for issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in {{input.Repo}} rejected by CEO. {{vars.approval_signal.Comment | default: ''}}

args.Subject: Issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} design spec in {{input.Repo}} (CEO review)
args.ReviewPrompt:
Review the design spec for GitHub issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in repo {{input.Repo}}. Fetch it with: gh issue view {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} --repo {{input.Repo}}

The CEO has reviewed this spec and requested changes before approving:
{{vars.approval_signal.Comment | default: 'no specific comment'}}

Evaluate whether the CEO's concerns are valid and warrant changes to the spec. If the spec already addresses the concern or the concern is not blocking, you may approve. If changes are needed, describe specifically what the author should change.

Review discipline:
- Fetch the current spec/PR fresh — do NOT summarize prior rounds.
- Verify each prior finding against the current text before reporting.
- Give structured findings with severity (blocker, high, medium, low).
- End your response with exactly one verdict line: VERDICT: approved | changes_requested | needs_human_review.

steps.name: renotify_ceo_reviewers_approve
steps.instruction:
Post to Telegram group: Design spec for issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in {{input.Repo}} — consensus reviewers evaluated your concern and still approve the spec.

Reviewer reasoning:
{{vars.ceo_consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning}}

Please re-evaluate and send a new 'design-approval' signal.

steps.name: notify_ceo_needs_human
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: DesignWorkflow for issue {{vars.issue_result | extract: 'ISSUE_NUMBER:\s*(\d+)'}} in {{input.Repo}} — CEO-triggered consensus review flagged needs_human_review. Manual intervention required.

steps.name: emit_ceo_needs_human_error
steps.instruction: Output exactly: ERROR:ceo_needs_human_review

workflowDefinitions.name: UweDocMaintenanceWorkflow
workflowDefinitions.description: Post-merge documentation maintenance. Reviews PR diff, updates project contexts/memory/instructions, gates on CEO doc-review signal. Max 2 revision rounds with cleanup on exhaustion.

steps.instruction:
Review the merged PR diff for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} on branch `{{input.BranchName}}` in repo {{input.Repo}} and determine whether any project documentation needs updating.

Merge summary: {{input.MergeSummary}}

Steps:
1. Fetch the diff: `gh pr diff {{input.PrNumber}} --repo {{input.Repo}}`
2. Check whether the changes touch: architecture, config, ports, services, schemas, tool mappings, docker config, CI/CD, or infrastructure.
3. If no doc-relevant changes — respond with exactly: NO_DOC_UPDATES_NEEDED
4. Otherwise, list what needs updating across:
   - project contexts in orchestrator DB (update_project_context MCP tool — no PR needed)
   - fleet-memory entries (memory_store / memory_update — no PR needed)
   - agent instructions in orchestrator DB (update_instruction MCP tool — no PR needed)
   - operational context memories: search fleet-memory for relevant WorkflowType operational context entries and list any that need updating (new gotchas, changed behavior, updated limits)

Based on the impact assessment below, update the relevant documentation for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} on branch `{{input.BranchName}}` in repo {{input.Repo}}.

What needs updating:
{{vars.assess_result}}

Actions:
1. Project context changes: use list_project_contexts to check current versions, then update_project_context to create a new version. Do NOT reprovision agents yet — that happens after CEO approval.
2. fleet-memory entries: update directly using memory_store / memory_update. Include operational context memories (titled 'WorkflowType — operational context') — add new gotchas, behavioral changes, or updated limits introduced by this PR.
3. Agent instruction changes: use list_instruction_versions to check current content, then update_instruction to create a new version. Do NOT reprovision agents yet.

At the end of your response, output BOTH lines in exactly this format:
DOC_PRS: org/repo#N [org/repo#M ...]  or  DOC_PRS: none
INSTRUCTION_UPDATES: instruction_name1 [instruction_name2 ...]  or  INSTRUCTION_UPDATES: none

notifyStep.instruction:
Post to the Telegram group:

Doc maintenance complete for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} in {{input.Repo}} (round 1/2).

PR: {{input.Repo}} #{{input.PrNumber}}
Merge summary: {{input.MergeSummary}}

Changes made:
{{vars.update_round1}}

Decision impact:
- approved: merges all doc PRs and reprovisioning agents — takes effect immediately
- changes_requested: agent revises docs (1 more round available)
- rejected: closes all doc PRs and rolls back all instruction versions

Please review and signal the workflow with 'doc-review':
  {"Decision":"approved"} — merge all doc PRs + reprovision agents with updated instructions
  {"Decision":"changes_requested","Comment":"your feedback"} — request revisions (1 more round available)
  {"Decision":"rejected","Comment":"reason"} — close all doc PRs + rollback instruction versions

steps.instruction:
The CEO approved the doc maintenance changes for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} in {{input.Repo}}.

Full round-1 update result (contains DOC_PRS and INSTRUCTION_UPDATES lines):
{{vars.update_round1}}

Actions:
1. Find the DOC_PRS line. For each listed PR (format org/repo#N): `gh pr merge <N> --repo <org/repo> --squash --delete-branch`. Skip if value is 'none'.
2. Find the INSTRUCTION_UPDATES line. For each listed instruction: use list_instruction_versions to confirm which agents use it, then call reprovision_agent for each affected agent. Skip if value is 'none'.

Report which PRs were merged and which agents were reprovisioned.

The CEO rejected the doc maintenance changes for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} in {{input.Repo}}.

CEO decision signal (parse for Comment): {{vars.doc_review_1}}

Full round-1 update result (contains DOC_PRS and INSTRUCTION_UPDATES lines):
{{vars.update_round1}}

Actions:
1. Find the DOC_PRS line. For each listed PR: `gh pr close <N> --repo <org/repo> --delete-branch`. Skip if 'none'.
2. Find the INSTRUCTION_UPDATES line. For each listed instruction: use list_instruction_versions to find the version created during this workflow, then use rollback_instruction to revert to the prior version. Skip if 'none'.

Report which PRs were closed and which instructions were rolled back.

Apply the CEO's feedback on the documentation changes for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} in {{input.Repo}} (revision round 2/2 — final).

CEO decision signal (parse for Comment): {{vars.doc_review_1}}

Round-1 update result (contains DOC_PRS and INSTRUCTION_UPDATES lines):
{{vars.update_round1}}

Actions:
1. For project context changes: use update_project_context to create a revised version addressing CEO feedback.
2. For fleet-memory changes: update entries directly.
3. For instruction changes: use update_instruction to create a revised version — do NOT reprovision yet.

At the end of your response, output this line:
INSTRUCTION_UPDATES: instruction_name1 [instruction_name2 ...]  or  INSTRUCTION_UPDATES: none

(DOC_PRS are the same as round 1 — no new PRs are opened in revision.)

Report what was changed.

notifyStep.instruction:
Post to the Telegram group:

Doc maintenance revision complete for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} in {{input.Repo}} (round 2/2 — final).

PR: {{input.Repo}} #{{input.PrNumber}}

Changes made in revision:
{{vars.update_round2}}

Decision impact:
- approved: merges all doc PRs and reprovisioning agents
- rejected: closes all doc PRs and rolls back all instruction versions
(Note: this is the final round — any further changes_requested triggers cleanup without another revision)

Please review and signal the workflow with 'doc-review':
  {"Decision":"approved"} — merge all doc PRs + reprovision agents
  {"Decision":"rejected","Comment":"reason"} — close all doc PRs + rollback
  (Note: this is the final round — any further changes_requested will trigger cleanup without another revision)

steps.instruction:
The CEO approved the doc maintenance changes for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} in {{input.Repo}} (after revision).

Round-1 update result (DOC_PRS and round-1 INSTRUCTION_UPDATES):
{{vars.update_round1}}

Round-2 update result (additional INSTRUCTION_UPDATES from revision, if any):
{{vars.update_round2}}

Actions:
1. Find the DOC_PRS line in round-1 result. For each listed PR: `gh pr merge <N> --repo <org/repo> --squash --delete-branch`. Skip if 'none'.
2. Find INSTRUCTION_UPDATES in BOTH round-1 and round-2 results. Combine the lists (dedup by name). For each instruction: use list_instruction_versions to confirm which agents use it, then call reprovision_agent for each affected agent. Skip if both are 'none'.

Report which PRs were merged and which agents were reprovisioned.

Doc maintenance for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} in {{input.Repo}} has exhausted both revision rounds without a final CEO decision (timeout or unexpected signal).

Round-1 update result (DOC_PRS and round-1 INSTRUCTION_UPDATES):
{{vars.update_round1}}

Round-2 update result (additional INSTRUCTION_UPDATES from revision, if any):
{{vars.update_round2}}

Actions — clean up dangling changes:
1. Find the DOC_PRS line in round-1 result. For each listed PR: `gh pr close <N> --repo <org/repo> --delete-branch`. Skip if 'none'.
2. Find INSTRUCTION_UPDATES in BOTH round-1 and round-2 results. Combine the lists (dedup by name). For each instruction: use rollback_instruction to revert to the pre-workflow version. Skip if both are 'none'.

Post to Telegram group: Doc maintenance for PR #{{input.PrNumber}} in {{input.Repo}} timed out after 2 rounds. Open PRs closed and instruction versions rolled back. Manual review may be needed.

workflowDefinitions.name: UwePrImplementationWorkflow
workflowDefinitions.description: Implements a GitHub issue on a branch, opens PR, runs consensus review loop (max 5 rounds), CEO merge-approval gate (max 5 rounds with changes_requested loop), merges, and fires DocMaintenanceWorkflow. Replaces compiled PrImplementationWorkflow.

steps.name: phase1_implement
steps.instruction:
Before acting, honor the memory hint that will be appended to this message — search fleet-memory for operational context keyed to this workflow type.

Implement the following task in repo {{input.Repo}}. When done, open a PR against main.

Task:
  Issue number: {{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}}
  Description: {{input.Description | default: 'none'}}

Branch name: Use `{{input.BranchName}}` if it is provided and non-empty. Otherwise, auto-generate: use `feat/issue-{{input.IssueNumber}}` if an issue number is provided, or a short `feat/<description-slug>` for description-only tasks.

IMPORTANT: If an issue number is provided, always fetch the latest issue details before starting — run `gh issue view {{input.IssueNumber}} --repo {{input.Repo}} --comments` as your first step and use THAT content as your spec.

At the end of your response, output the PR URL on its own line:
PR_URL: https://github.com/{{input.Repo}}/pull/NNN

args.Subject: PR for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}}

notifyStep.instruction:
Post to Telegram group: PR for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} ({{vars.pr_url}}) needs your human review decision.

Consensus flagged needs_human_review:
{{vars.consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning}}

Send the 'human-review' signal to this workflow:
  {"Decision":"approved"} — approve and proceed to merge
  {"Decision":"changes_requested","Comment":"feedback"} — send back for revision
  {"Decision":"rejected"} — cancel the workflow

steps.name: notify_human_review_timeout
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: PrImplementationWorkflow for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} auto-cancelled: no human-review response after 3 hours.

steps.name: notify_merge_rejected
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: PR for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} ({{vars.pr_url}}) rejected by human reviewer. Reason: {{vars.human_decision.Comment | default: 'no reason given'}}

default.name: revise_human_feedback
default.instruction:
Before acting, honor the memory hint that will be appended to this message — search fleet-memory for operational context keyed to this workflow type.

Apply the following human reviewer feedback on the PR in repo {{input.Repo}}:

Feedback: {{vars.human_decision.Comment | default: 'changes requested'}}

Prior consensus feedback:
{{vars.consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning | default: 'none'}}

When incorporating prior consensus or CEO feedback:
- Treat the comment as authoritative. Do not re-argue the design.
- Restate the concern in your own words in a comment on the issue/PR, so reviewers can verify you understood.
- Make the change the comment asks for. If you believe it is wrong, respond via changes_requested on the next round rather than ignoring it.

default.name: revise_consensus_feedback
default.instruction:
Before acting, honor the memory hint that will be appended to this message — search fleet-memory for operational context keyed to this workflow type.

Revise the PR in repo {{input.Repo}} based on review feedback.

Full feedback (apply all):
{{vars.consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning}}

Prior CEO feedback (if any):
{{vars.ceo_consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning | default: 'none'}}

When incorporating prior consensus or CEO feedback:
- Treat the comment as authoritative. Do not re-argue the design.
- Restate the concern in your own words in a comment on the issue/PR, so reviewers can verify you understood.
- Make the change the comment asks for. If you believe it is wrong, respond via changes_requested on the next round rather than ignoring it.

steps.name: notify_consensus_exhausted
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: PrImplementationWorkflow for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} exhausted 5 consensus rounds without approval. Manual intervention required.

steps.name: phase2_5_unresolved_check
steps.instruction:
Before acting, honor the memory hint that will be appended to this message — search fleet-memory for operational context keyed to this workflow type.

Check the PR at {{vars.pr_url}} in repo {{input.Repo}} for any unresolved review comments (use `gh pr view --json reviews,reviewRequests` and `gh api repos/{{input.Repo}}/pulls` to inspect). If there are unresolved inline comments or review threads, address each one — push any fixes to the branch. If there are none, confirm all review comments have been addressed. Report: either 'no unresolved comments' or a summary of what was fixed.

notifyStep.instruction:
Post to Telegram group: PR for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in repo {{input.Repo}} ({{vars.pr_url}}) has been approved by consensus and is ready to merge.

Context for your decision:
- PR: {{vars.pr_url}}
- Consensus: reviewers approved the implementation as correct and complete
- Consensus reasoning: {{vars.consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning | default: 'see PR review'}}
- approved → PR is merged immediately and doc maintenance runs; changes_requested → re-enters consensus review loop; rejected → PR is closed without merging

Send the 'merge-approval' signal to this workflow:
  {"Decision":"approved"} — approve and merge
  {"Decision":"changes_requested","Comment":"feedback"} — request changes before merging
  {"Decision":"rejected","Comment":"reason"} — reject and close without merging

steps.name: notify_merge_timeout
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: PrImplementationWorkflow for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} auto-cancelled: no merge-approval response after 3 hours.

steps.name: notify_merge_rejected
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: PR for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} ({{vars.pr_url}}) rejected by CEO at merge gate. Reason: {{vars.merge_decision.Comment | default: 'no reason given'}}

args.Subject: CEO merge-gate feedback on PR for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}}
args.ReviewPrompt:
Review the PR at {{vars.pr_url}} in repo {{input.Repo}}.

The CEO has reviewed the approved PR and requested changes before merging:
{{vars.merge_decision.Comment | default: 'no specific comment'}}

Evaluate whether the CEO's concerns are valid and warrant code changes.
Implementation summary:
{{vars.impl_result}}

Prior review history:
{{vars.consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning | default: 'none'}}

Review discipline:
- Fetch the current spec/PR fresh — do NOT summarize prior rounds.
- Verify each prior finding against the current text before reporting.
- Give structured findings with severity (blocker, high, medium, low).
- End your response with exactly one verdict line: VERDICT: approved | changes_requested | needs_human_review.

changes_requested.name: revise_ceo_feedback
changes_requested.instruction:
Before acting, honor the memory hint that will be appended to this message — search fleet-memory for operational context keyed to this workflow type.

The CEO requested changes before merging the PR in repo {{input.Repo}}.

CEO feedback: {{vars.merge_decision.Comment | default: 'no specific comment'}}

Reviewers have confirmed the CEO's concerns are valid. Apply the necessary changes and push to the branch.

Reviewer consensus on CEO feedback:
{{vars.ceo_consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning}}

Prior feedback:
{{vars.consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning | default: 'none'}}

When incorporating prior consensus or CEO feedback:
- Treat the comment as authoritative. Do not re-argue the design.
- Restate the concern in your own words in a comment on the issue/PR, so reviewers can verify you understood.
- Make the change the comment asks for. If you believe it is wrong, respond via changes_requested on the next round rather than ignoring it.

steps.name: notify_ceo_consensus_needs_human
steps.instruction: Post to Telegram group: PrImplementationWorkflow for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} — CEO-triggered consensus review flagged needs_human_review. Manual intervention required.

default.name: renotify_ceo_reviewers_approve
default.instruction:
Post to Telegram group: Reviewers have evaluated the CEO's feedback on PR for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} and concluded the changes are not warranted.

Reviewer reasoning:
{{vars.ceo_consensus_out.ConsolidatedReasoning}}

Please re-evaluate and send another 'merge-approval' signal.

default.name: notify_outer_loop_exhausted
default.instruction: Post to Telegram group: PrImplementationWorkflow for issue #{{input.IssueNumber | default: 'N/A'}} in {{input.Repo}} ({{vars.pr_url}}) has exhausted all 5 outer merge-gate rounds without final approval or rejection. Manual intervention required — check the PR and Temporal dashboard.

steps.name: phase4_merge
steps.instruction: Merge the PR at {{vars.pr_url}} in repo {{input.Repo}}. Do not force-push. Confirm once merged.

steps.name: verify_merge_status
steps.instruction:
Verify the PR at {{vars.pr_url}} in repo {{input.Repo}} was actually merged. Run: gh pr view $(echo '{{vars.pr_url}}' | grep -o '[0-9]*$') --repo {{input.Repo}} --json state,merged

If the state is MERGED, output exactly: MERGED
If not merged (open, closed without merge, or any error), output exactly: FAILED

default.name: notify_merge_failed
